skillDirectoryLayout:
Hermes skill directory layout (single source of truth, per /Users/kiscsicska/.hermes/hermes-agent/tools/skill_manager_tool.py:107-109 and /Users/kiscsicska/.hermes/hermes-agent/tools/skills_tool.py:90-91):

Local user skills (created by `skill_manage(action='create')`):
    ~/.hermes/skills/
    ├── my-skill/                  # bare name (no category)
    │   ├── SKILL.md               # REQUIRED, frontmatter + body
    │   ├── references/            # *.md supporting docs (ALLOWED_SUBDIRS)
    │   ├── templates/             # *.md, *.py, *.yaml, *.json, *.tex, *.sh
    │   ├── scripts/               # *.py, *.sh, *.bash, *.js, *.ts, *.rb
    │   └── assets/                # agentskills.io standard; arbitrary files
    └── category-name/             # optional grouping subdir
        └── another-skill/
            └── SKILL.md

In-repo (bundled) skills (per /Users/kiscsicska/.hermes/hermes-agent/skills/software-development/hermes-agent-skill-authoring/SKILL.md:18-27, 97-101):
    skills/<category>/<skill-name>/SKILL.md
    Categories present in repo: autonomous-ai-agents, creative, data-science, devops, dogfood, email, gaming, github, leisure, mcp, media, mlops/*, note-taking, productivity, red-teaming, research, smart-home, social-media, software-development.

Canonical on-disk name is SKILL.md (uppercase, exact). Optional subdirs: {references, templates, scripts, assets} (skill_manager_tool.py:171, ALLOWED_SUBDIRS).

Plugin-bundled skills (per /Users/kiscsicska/.hermes/hermes-agent/tools/skills_tool.py:752-856 and /Users/kiscsicska/.hermes/hermes-agent/agent/skill_utils.py:678-695): referenced by qualified name `namespace:skill-name` where namespace matches `^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$`; bare name = local skill; categorized form `category/skill` falls through to on-disk path.
manifestFields: name (REQUIRED, enforced) — matches ^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9._-]*$, max 64 chars
description (REQUIRED, enforced) — max 1024 chars; should start with "Use when …" per peer convention
version (optional, peer convention) — semver string
author (optional, peer convention) — string
license (optional, peer convention) — typically MIT
platforms (optional) — list of [macos, linux, windows]; absent = all platforms (default)
environments (optional) — list of [kanban, docker, s6]; absent = all; offer-time filter only
prerequisites.env_vars (legacy) — list; normalized into required_environment_variables on load
prerequisites.commands (legacy) — list; advisory only
required_environment_variables (optional) — list of {name, prompt, help?, required_for?, optional?}
required_credential_files (optional) — list of paths
setup.help (optional) — string shown when setup needed
setup.collect_secrets (optional) — list of {env_var, prompt, provider_url?, secret?}
compatibility (optional, agentskills.io) — string
metadata.hermes.tags (peer convention) — list of short descriptive strings
metadata.hermes.related_skills (peer convention) — list of in-repo skill names (user-local references break for fresh clones)
metadata.hermes.requires_toolsets (optional) — list
metadata.hermes.fallback_for_toolsets (optional) — list
metadata.hermes.requires_tools (optional) — list
metadata.hermes.fallback_for_tools (optional) — list
metadata.hermes.config (optional) — list of {key, description, default, prompt}
Body section pattern (peer convention, not enforced) — # Title → ## Overview → ## When to Use → body → ## Common Pitfalls → ## Verification Checklist
Frontmatter delimiters (enforced) — file starts at byte 0 with ---; closes with \n---\n; YAML must parse to a mapping; non-empty body after closing ---
File size (enforced) — total SKILL.md content <= 100,000 chars (MAX_SKILL_CONTENT_CHARS); per supporting file <= 1,048,576 bytes (MAX_SKILL_FILE_BYTES)
Supporting-file subdirs (enforced) — must be under {references, templates, scripts, assets}; no `..` traversal; SKILL.md accepted only as bare or one-level path
allowedAndForbiddenInvocations:
Hermes tool names that replace Claude Code tool names referenced in the Anthropic skill-creator. Source: /Users/kiscsicska/.hermes/hermes-agent/tools/registry.py and grep of all registry.register(name=...) calls in /Users/kiscsicska/.hermes/hermes-agent/tools/*.py.

Claude tool name -> Hermes tool name:
- Skill (Claude) -> skill_manage (for create/edit/patch/delete/write_file/remove_file) AND skill_view / skills_list (for reading). Registered in tools/skill_manager_tool.py:1109 and tools/skills_tool.py:1574,1609.
- Read (Claude) -> read_file (tools/file_tools.py:1514). For linked-file subresources also skill_view(name, file_path=...).
- Write (Claude) -> write_file (tools/file_tools.py:1528). Atomic create/overwrite of any file.
- Edit (Claude) -> patch (tools/file_tools.py:1545). mode='replace' (default) with old_string/new_string; mode='patch' for V4A multi-file patches.
- Glob (Claude) -> search_files with target='files' (tools/file_tools.py:1596; "Find files by glob pattern... use this instead of ls — results sorted by modification time").
- Grep (Claude) -> search_files with target='content' (ripgrep-backed).
- Bash (Claude) -> terminal (tools/terminal_tool.py; "Use this for ANY system state mutations"). For read-only shell inspection prefer read_terminal (tools/read_terminal_tool.py:83) or execute_code for sandboxed Python (tools/code_execution_tool.py:1838).
- AskUserQuestion (Claude) -> clarify (tools/clarify_tool.py; registered as name="clarify"). Also: cronjob (one-shot reminders) and todo (multi-step todos).
- Task / subagent (Claude) -> delegate_task (tools/delegate_tool.py) for subagent dispatch; mixture_of_agents (tools/mixture_of_agents_tool.py) for MoA routing.
- WebSearch (Claude) -> web_search (tools/web_tools.py:1367) and web_extract (tools/web_tools.py).
- WebFetch (Claude) -> web_extract (tools/web_tools.py).
- TodoWrite (Claude) -> todo (tools/todo_tool.py:301).
- NotebookEdit / Excel / Word reading (Claude Read variants) -> read_file auto-extracts .ipynb/.docx/.xlsx to readable text (tools/file_tools.py:1515 description).
- Image content in prompt (Claude vision) -> vision_analyze (tools/vision_tools.py).
- Schedule a deferred task (Claude CronCreate/CronDelete/CronList) -> cronjob (tools/cronjob_tools.py:869). Single tool with action dispatch.
- Memory / persistent notes (no Claude equivalent) -> memory (tools/memory_tool.py:796).
- Code execution sandbox (no direct Claude equivalent; Claude uses Bash) -> execute_code (tools/code_execution_tool.py:1838).
- Process management (no Claude equivalent) -> process (tools/process_registry.py:1667).
- Long-running output readback -> read_terminal (tools/read_terminal_tool.py:83).
- Image generation (no Claude equivalent here) -> image_generate (tools/image_generation_tool.py:1172).
- Video generation / analysis -> video_generate (tools/video_generation_tool.py:553), video_analyze.
- Speech synthesis / transcription -> text_to_speech, voice_mode.
- Browser automation (browser_* family) -> browser_navigate, browser_click, browser_type, browser_snapshot, browser_console, browser_back, browser_scroll, browser_press, browser_vision, browser_get_images, browser_dialog, browser_cdp (tools/browser_tool.py, browser_dialog_tool.py, browser_cdp_tool.py).
- Kanban workflow -> kanban_create, kanban_list, kanban_show, kanban_comment, kanban_link, kanban_block, kanban_unblock, kanban_complete, kanban_heartbeat (tools/kanban_tools.py).
- Cross-session search -> session_search (tools/session_search_tool.py).
- Cross-user messaging -> send_message (tools/send_message_tool.py:1905); discord, discord_admin (tools/discord_tool.py); x_search (tools/x_search_tool.py); yuanbao_* (tools/yuanbao_tools.py); feishu_* (tools/feishu_doc_tool.py, tools/feishu_drive_tool.py); homeassistant ha_* (tools/homeassistant_tool.py); computer_use (tools/computer_use_tool.py).
- MCP-prefixed tool calls (this session) -> the same underlying Hermes tools; the mcp__<server>__ prefix is session-routing, the canonical Hermes name is what follows.

FORBIDDEN in migrated skill: any bare `Read`/`Write`/`Edit`/`Glob`/`Grep`/`Bash`/`Task`/`Skill`/`AskUserQuestion`/`WebSearch`/`WebFetch`/`TodoWrite`/`NotebookEdit`/`CronCreate`/`CronDelete`/`CronList` references must be translated to the Hermes names above. The skill MUST NOT call Claude-Code-only tools like `EnterPlanMode`/`ExitPlanMode` (use Hermes `delegate_task`/`mixture_of_agents` for dispatch and prose for planning).
authoringValidatorRules:
What hermes-agent-skill-authoring enforces (source of truth: /Users/kiscsicska/.hermes/hermes-agent/skills/software-development/hermes-agent-skill-authoring/SKILL.md combined with /Users/kiscsicska/.hermes/hermes-agent/tools/skill_manager_tool.py::_validate_frontmatter and _validate_name/_validate_category/_validate_file_path/_validate_content_size):

HARD RULES (machine-checked, action will be rejected):
1. SKILL.md must start with `---` at byte 0 — no leading blank line, no BOM. (skill_manager_tool.py:225)
2. Closing delimiter must be `\n---\n` (regex `\n---\s*\n` after offset 3). (skill_manager_tool.py:228)
3. YAML frontmatter must parse cleanly (yaml.safe_load). (skill_manager_tool.py:234-237)
4. Top-level YAML must be a mapping. (skill_manager_tool.py:239-240)
5. `name` field required. (skill_manager_tool.py:242-243)
6. `description` field required, string-coerced. (skill_manager_tool.py:244-245)
7. `len(description) <= 1024` (MAX_DESCRIPTION_LENGTH). (skill_manager_tool.py:246-247)
8. Non-empty body after closing `---`. (skill_manager_tool.py:249-251)
9. `len(content) <= 100,000` chars (MAX_SKILL_CONTENT_CHARS). (skill_manager_tool.py:256-268, 164)
10. Skill name matches `^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9._-]*$` AND `len(name) <= 64` (MAX_NAME_LENGTH, VALID_NAME_RE). (skill_manager_tool.py:111, 168, 178-189)
11. Optional category, if present, must be a single directory segment with the same regex/length rules; no `/` or `\`. (skill_manager_tool.py:192-214)
12. Supporting files (write_file/remove_file/patch-with-file_path) must be under one of {references, templates, scripts, assets}; no `..` traversal; SKILL.md accepts only the bare or one-level form. (skill_manager_tool.py:401-435, ALLOWED_SUBDIRS line 171)
13. Per-supporting-file byte cap: 1,048,576 bytes (1 MiB) (MAX_SKILL_FILE_BYTES). (skill_manager_tool.py:165, 736-744)
14. Patch results are re-validated: frontmatter must remain intact after SKILL.md patches, and size limit must still hold on the patched result. (skill_manager_tool.py:640-652)
15. Pinned skills cannot be deleted (skill_usage pin guard) but can be patched/edited. (skill_manager_tool.py:137-161, applied at line 685)
16. absorbed_into target for `delete` must already exist and not equal the deleted name. (skill_manager_tool.py:690-705)
17. `absorbed_into=""` is the explicit "no forwarding target" signal; omitting is accepted for back-compat but logs a warning.
18. Skill-write approval gate: by default, agent-initiated create/edit/patch/delete/write_file/remove_file pass through; with `skills.guard_agent_created=true` a security scan (tools/skills_guard.py) runs and can roll back. (skill_manager_tool.py:59-102, 522-525, 562-566, 658-661, 763-769)
19. Skills live under ~/.hermes/skills/ (single source of truth); bundled in-repo skills are seeded on install but are NOT targetable by `skill_manage(action='create')`. (skill_manager_tool.py:107-109; SKILL.md lines 18-21)
20. The session's skill loader is cached at start — newly created skills are not visible in the current session; verify in a fresh session or via `skill_view` with the explicit path. (SKILL.md line 126, 151)

SOFT RULES (peer-matching conventions, not enforced but every in-repo peer satisfies them — the validator skill itself models the template at /Users/kiscsicska/.hermes/hermes-agent/skills/software-development/hermes-agent-skill-authoring/SKILL.md:42-54):
- Top-level fields: version, author, license.
- `metadata.hermes.tags` (list of short descriptive strings) and `metadata.hermes.related_skills` (in-repo skills only; user-local references break for fresh clones — SKILL.md line 130, 153).
- Description starts with "Use when …" and describes a trigger class, not a one-shot task. (SKILL.md line 144)
- Body sections: # Title → ## Overview → ## When to Use → body → ## Common Pitfalls → ## Verification Checklist (minimum). (SKILL.md lines 67-93, 162)
- File size 8–15k chars for in-repo skills; if >20k, split into references/*.md and link from SKILL.md. (SKILL.md line 62)
- Cross-skill links go through `metadata.hermes.related_skills`, resolved across both trees at load. (agent/skill_utils.py is the resolver)
- Category choice: pick the closest existing top-level category, do not invent new ones casually. (SKILL.md line 103)

ADDITIONAL VALIDATION (not in the validator skill, but observed in the loader):
- platform matching (skill_manager_tool.py → tools/skills_tool.py → agent/skill_utils.py: skill_matches_platform) — top-level `platforms:` list; mapped via PLATFORM_MAP {macos→darwin, linux→linux, windows→win32}; Termux treated as linux/android compat (lines 21-26, 128-169).
- environment matching — top-level `environments:` list, offer-time filter only; explicit skill_view/--skills bypass (lines 182-269).
- Injection pattern detection on skill content load — log warning, still serve (tools/skills_tool.py:160-171, 1127-1138).
- Disabled-skill set read from config.yaml `skills.disabled` and `skills.platform_disabled.<platform>` (lines 318-353, 573-596).
- Out-of-tree skill load triggers a "outside trusted skills directory" warning (tools/skills_tool.py:1111-1138).
